articles.title: Momentum vs pressure: Which teams are peaking at the right time in 2026 FIFA World Cup?
articles.description: The FIFA World Cup knockout stage is here, with teams showing diverse forms. France, Brazil, and Spain are setting a strong pace with dynamic play. Argentina, Germany, and England are displaying composure under pressure, while Portugal and the Netherlands are quietly peaking. Success now hinges on adapting and performing when it matters most, balancing momentum with calm execution.

articles.title: Parenting advice of the day: Experts say this one decision can become the biggest investment in your child’s future
articles.description: An ex-Army officer suggests enrolling children in sports under proper guidance is a crucial investment for their future. Beyond physical fitness, structured sports training cultivates mental strength, resilience, discipline, and consistency. This approach simplifies parenting by channeling children's energy positively, reducing screen time and everyday conflicts, ultimately shaping them into well-rounded individuals.

articles.title: ‘A bit harsh’: Manjrekar raises questions after Jaiswal’s exclusion despite strong ODI returns
articles.description: Debate erupts over Yashasvi Jaiswal's omission from the ODI team following his remarkable century. Sanjay Manjrekar, a former player, critiques this 'harsh' decision, questioning the selection committee's long-term strategy for Rohit Sharma's future. He argues for merit-based selections and stresses the importance of nurturing young talent like Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, asserting that the focus should be on what benefits Indian cricket as a whole.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Didier Deschamps to miss France's Norway clash after mother's death
articles.description: France's World Cup campaign faces a personal blow as coach Didier Deschamps will miss the Norway match following his mother's passing. He has returned to France for the funeral, with assistant Guy Stéphan stepping in. This comes as Deschamps prepares to depart after a highly successful 14-year tenure, having led France to a World Cup title and another final. The team has already secured a knockout stage spot.

articles.title: "Harder to get answers": Dianna Russini’s departure creates unexpected roadblock in Mike Vrabel investigation, says NFL insider Mike Florio
articles.description: The Athletic's investigation into Dianna Russini's alleged relationship with Patriots coach Mike Vrabel is still ongoing, but it faces mounting challenges. Russini's departure from the outlet has made cooperation unlikely, while the publication's commitment to employee transparency risks leaks. As NFL insider Mike Florio noted, any public disclosure of findings could invite a defamation lawsuit from Russini, leaving The Athletic navigating a difficult legal and editorial tightrope with no clear exit.

articles.title: Croatia stay alive at FIFA World Cup 2026, Budimir strike knocks Panama out
articles.description: Croatia edged a tense contest. Ante Budimir scored the only goal after the break. Panama pushed hard in the closing stages, but they could not find the finishing touch and are now out of the tournament.

articles.title: Team goals matter most: Cristiano Ronaldo breaks silence on records after 5-0 rout vs Uzbekistan
articles.description: After a record-breaking outing in which he scored two goals in the 5-0 rout of Uzbekistan in the FIFA World Cup 2026 in Houston, Portuguese legend Cristiano Ronaldo said what mattered more to him was that the team achieved its objectives.

articles.title: Cristiano Ronaldo’s fiancée Georgina Rodriguez shows off trouser-free celebration as Portugal secures its first victory
articles.description: Georgina Rodriguez celebrated Portugal’s first win of the 2026 World Cup by posting a photo on Instagram while watching Cristiano Ronaldo’s side defeat Uzbekistan 5-0. Rodriguez’s trouser-free matchday look drew attention online, but the major sporting story belonged to Ronaldo, who scored twice and became the first player to find the net in six different World Cups. The victory moved Portugal into a strong position in Group K ahead of its final group-stage match against Colombia.

articles.title: What is Norway's 'Viking Row'? Fans' viral World Cup celebration sends social media into a frenzy
articles.description: Norway's 'Viking Row' fan celebration has taken the FIFA World Cup by storm, captivating audiences worldwide. Inspired by Viking heritage, supporters and players alike perform synchronized rowing motions, symbolizing unity and a fighting spirit. This viral trend, seen from stadiums to New York City, highlights the team's strong performance, with Erling Haaland leading the charge.

articles.title: Stand-in Goalkeeper Frustrates England As Three Lions Play Goalless Draw Against Ghana In FIFA World Cup
articles.description: England dominated possession and had 19 shots to their opponents' two, yet rarely looked like scoring past Ghana's goalkeeper Benjamin Asare in a match watched by 63,983 fans.

articles.title: England stumble against fearless Ghana as Tuchel's side waste chance to seal knockout spot
articles.description: England struggled to a goalless draw with Ghana, failing to convert possession into scoring chances.

articles.title: England vs Ghana – Three Lions held to a goalless draw; player ratings
articles.description: A defensively astute performance by Ghana saw it hold England to a goalless draw in their Group L match at the Boston Stadium in Massachusetts on Tuesday.

articles.title: "Trying to force a kiss": Shakira's unexpected FIFA World Cup kiss sends social media into meltdown as fans fear the worst
articles.description: Shakira's appearance at Argentina's 2-0 FIFA World Cup win over Austria sparked online concern after a live broadcast showed someone kissing her on the cheek. Fans questioned the awkward moment before it was revealed that the person was her 13-year-old son, Milan, whose face was partially hidden by a cap. The revelation quickly calmed social media, while Argentina secured a knockout-stage berth thanks to Lionel Messi's brace.

articles.title: House of the Dragon star Matt Smith makes bold FIFA World Cup confession as England chases glory
articles.description: House of the Dragon star Matt Smith revealed he would rather see England win the FIFA World Cup than receive 10 Academy Awards. Speaking before England's Group L clash against Ghana, the actor said the country's football success means more to him than Hollywood recognition. England opened the tournament with a 4-2 win over Croatia and could secure a place in the knockout stage with another victory under Thomas Tuchel.

articles.title: "That guy's not coming back": Lamar Jackson's Ravens future grows more uncertain after former NFL scout's bold claim
articles.description: Former NFL scout John Middlekauff believes Lamar Jackson is no longer the explosive runner who dominated early in his career, saying, "That guy's not coming back." While he praised Jackson's growth as a passer, he stressed the Ravens must find the right balance between protecting their quarterback and maximizing his strengths. With Jackson's contract extension looming, Baltimore faces important decisions about his future and the team's long-term direction.

articles.title: Luka Modric Becomes 4th Footballer In The World To Play 200 International Matches
articles.description: Modrić is the first Croatian and only the fourth player in the world to play 200 or more international matches. He made his international debut on March 1, 2006.
